title: How options are compared in the viewer
question: How should an option's tradeoffs be shown — separately per option, or compared across options on shared criteria?
rationale: Show options as a criterion-aligned table — criteria down the left, options across the top, helps/hurts/mixed marks with short notes, chosen column tinted, open by default. Chosen because re-judging a call means comparing options on the same criterion, and 67% of criteria recur across options so rows align.

tradeoffs.note: read one criterion across every option — the core re-judging move
a column of ✓/✗/~ marks reads at a glance, like a pricing grid
blank cells where an option didn't weigh a criterion; 67% of criteria still align
four options get tight; the table scrolls sideways on narrow screens

options.label: Pros / Cons per option, folded (the old way)

tradeoffs.note: compact, one option at a time
can't compare options on the same axis; the comparison sat two clicks deep

options.label: Options side-by-side, not aligned by criterion

tradeoffs.note: no need to reconcile criteria across options
loses same-axis comparison, which is the whole point
